channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Outback council hopes sorghum crop will reduce pressure on ratepayers
item.description: An outback Queensland council grows sorghum in a bid to create new income streams, reduce pressure on ratepayers, and support jobs in the region.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Wilson

media:group.media:description: Sorghum grown near Maxwelton is part of Richmond Shire Council's push to create new income streams.

item.title: A dinner party for black tennis players continues a proud tradition
item.description: From the Harlem Renaissance of the 20s and 30s to Soul Food Sundays at Wimbledon in the 80s and 90s, Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's dinner party before the French Open continues a long history of black athletes coming together.

media:group.media:description: Coco Gauff, Gael Monfils, Naomi Osaka, Taylor Townsend, Asia Muhammad and Christopher Eubanks.

item.title: 'I won't be a yes man': Co-chair of ACT Reconciliation council resigns
item.description: ACT Reconciliation Council co-chair Selina Walker resigns, saying she has lost faith in the territory government's approach to Indigenous affairs.
item.dc:creator: James Vyver

media:group.media:description: Selina Walker says there has been a failure to take practical steps towards reconciliation.

item.title: Rockliff faces parliamentary grilling after Ogilvie resigns as minister
item.description: "You can ask it in 50 different ways … I won't be commenting any further", the Tasmanian premier says as he responds to questioning in parliament from Labor and the Greens.
item.dc:creator: Loretta Lohberger and Lucy MacDonald

media:group.media:description: Madeleine Ogilvie resigned from her cabinet position on Saturday, relinquishing her ministerial portfolios.

item.title: Greens stalwart Bob Brown joins fight against small South West sand mine
item.description: The prominent environmentalist declares the sand mine project "an industrial nightmare" after visiting the area.
item.dc:creator: Madigan Landry

media:group.media:description: Bob Brown visited the site this month.

item.title: Olympic stadium work underway as premier warns off protesters
item.description: The last protesters were removed from Victoria Park in the early hours of Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Williams

media:group.media:description: Police remain at Victoria Park, where hundreds protested the new stadium over the weekend.

item.title: Police in outback Queensland defend shooting injured dog
item.description: A North West Queensland woman says her family has been left traumatised after police shot her granddaughter's injured dog in a residential street.

media:group.media:description: Queensland Police have defended their handling of the incident.

item.title: Government loses bid to keep terror funding documents secret
item.description: Commissioner Bell said the documents would provide a comparison of the resourcing given to counterterrorism agencies before and after August 2024, when the terror treat level was raised to "probable".
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: Michelle Rowland has said it is standard procedure to request confidentiality for cabinet documents.

item.title: Large flock of rare swift parrots seen near Port Macquarie
item.description: The sighting of critically endangered parrots is seen as "really exciting" after hardly any were seen across NSW last year. But a bird expert warns the species still faces significant threats.
item.dc:creator: Emma Siossian

media:group.media:description: James Bennett has spotted a flock of swift parrots at Lake Cathie.

item.title: Fowler wins FA Cup with Man City as fans honour Aussie coach's late father
item.description: The Women's FA Cup has a distinctly Australian flavour with Mary Fowler's Manchester City thumping Charlize Rule's Brighton and Hove Albion, coached by compatriot Dario Vidošić, whose father was honoured by fans at Wembley.

media:group.media:description: Mary Fowler's Manchester City lifted the Women's FA Cup for the fourth time in the past decade.

item.title: Teen star reaches first major quarterfinal while Świątek out of French Open
item.description: Nineteen-year-old Joao Fonseca beats two-time French Open runner-up Casper Ruud to reach his first grand slam quarterfinal, while four-time champion Iga Świątek suffers her earliest exit since 2019.

media:group.media:description: Nineteen-year-old Joao Fonseca is into his first major quarterfinal.

item.title: Australia could still buy five US submarines if needed, defence minister says
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended a shake-up to AUKUS that would involve Australia buying three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Defence Minister Richard Marles has indicated Australia could still move to purchase two more submarines from the United States if needed.

item.title: Almost 70,000 without power after storms tear through southern WA
item.description: Tens of thousands of homes are without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roof torn off one Perth apartment building, after WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.
item.dc:creator: Callum Liddelow

media:group.media:description: Quindalup boat ramp in WA's South West was left inundated as sea levels rose in the severe weather.

item.title: Aussie Hindley on Giro podium as great Dane Vingegaard completes set
item.description: Australian Jai Hindley finishes third at the Giro d'Italia, while Denmark's Jonas Vingegaard adds a title in Rome to his victories in France and Spain.

media:group.media:description: Jai Hindley (right) has now finished first, second and third at the Giro d'Italia.

item.title: Alarm as nursing cuts leave High Country town without after-hours care
item.description: Swifts Creek is an hour-and-a-half drive from the nearest hospital and residents are worried cuts to after-hours care at its Bush Nursing Centre might leave the community at risk.
item.dc:creator: Zaida Glibanovic

media:group.media:description: Sue Carroll with Deborah Martinez and her daughter.

item.title: Cattle company's own tip-off revealed biosecurity breach
item.description: A NSW company tipped off authorities to the illegal movement of its cattle, triggering the investigation that led to the largest-ever biosecurity fine under a Queensland law.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Long and Aisling Brennan

media:group.media:description: A biosecurity officer inspects cattle.

item.title: Live: ASX lower despite Wall St gains, AUD flat, oil higher
item.description: The Australian share market has begun winter with a slight decline, with the ASX 200 in negative territory to start the new trading week. The weakness comes despite a solid finish on Wall Street on Friday, with the Dow Jones, S&P 500 and Nasdaq all ending higher. Follow our live business blog.
item.dc:creator: Jason Dasey

item.title: Inhumane conditions at ICE detention camp, lawsuit says
item.description: Civil rights groups allege people at the largest immigration detention centre in Texas are being beaten, abused and denied medical care at the camp.

media:group.media:description: An aerial view shows the ICE detention facility in El Paso.

item.title: Live: Pauline Hanson says she has 'the ability' to be PM
item.description: Pauline Hanson says she has the ability to lead the nation as prime minister but admits she was shocked by a poll that suggested One Nation had pulled ahead of Labor. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: One Nation South Australian leader Cory Bernardi and federal leader Pauline Hanson at South Australia's Parliament House on May 5, 2026, for the opening of parliament.

item.title: Could native forests be part of Cement Australia's 'sustainable' fuel option?
item.description: Used tyres and wood waste will soon help to power a large cement manufacturing site in Tasmania as Cement Australia moves away from coal to alternative fuels, but some remain concerned the environmental benefit may be offset if the wood waste is sourced from logged native forests.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Reid

media:group.media:description: Environmentalists and the Greens are questioning whether the federal funding will assist in increasing emissions by supporting native logging rather than its intended purpose of decreasing carbon emissions.

item.title: 'Bit of a gold rush' as companies search for underground hydrogen
item.description: It's been about for a century, but companies are springing to action to find "gold hydrogen" underground around the country, as Australia and the world strive to reach net zero emissions by 2050.
item.dc:creator: Josh Brine

media:group.media:description: Gold Hydrogen's been exploring for natural hydrogen in South Australia.

item.title: Officeworks plan to offshore white-collar jobs 'short-term solution'
item.description: The company says it wants a "globally connected team", but disgruntled staff say plans to offshore jobs is about replacing them with cheap labour.
item.dc:creator: Isobel Roe

media:group.media:description: Officeworks is set to replace its Sydney customer service staff with a call centre in the Philippines.

item.title: Two new faces named as Socceroos reveal World Cup squad
item.description: Recently realigned Cristian Volpato and Japan-born Tete Yengi are in line for potential national debuts after being named in Australia's squad for this year's World Cup.

media:group.media:description: Tony Popovic has 17 potential World Cup debutants in his team.

item.title: US Navy interested in Darwin ship lift, NT minister says
item.description: Northern Territory Infrastructure Minister Bill Yan says the United States Navy has expressed interest in using the controversial Darwin ship lift, which is expected to be operational next year.
item.dc:creator: Jack Hislop

media:group.media:description: The US Navy is watching the progress on the Darwin ship lift to see if it will suit its needs.

item.title: Supreme Leader hard to find as Trump wants peace plan rewrites
item.description: Donald Trump is reported to be amending a peace plan, which could fuel further delays in finding a final agreement.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran

media:group.media:description: ABC Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran in Jerusalem
